events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to produce a JSON answer for a concrete/masonry flashing and weeps scenario. The observed condition: "flashing not lapped/sealed". Work stage: rough-complete. Component condition: installed-defective. We need to decide pass/fail/needs_more_info, risk, etc. Flashing not lapped/sealed is a defect that can lead to water intrusion, so it's a fail. Risk: high or critical? Flashing defects can cause moisture damage, so high. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: ["installed-defective"]. Percent complete: maybe 80%? Rough-complete means installed but not closed out, so maybe 80-90%. Value: not applicable, set 0. Sound source: not asked, set empty string. Confidence: high, 0.9. Event time, rate: not applicable, 0. Order: not asked, empty list. Workable: not asked, empty list. Findings: short defect description. Actions: immediate corrective action: re-lap and seal flashing. Rationale: brief. References: TMS 402, BIA tech notes. So output JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Flashing not lapped or sealed, creating a direct path for water intrusion behind the masonry veneer."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove affected flashing, properly lap shingle-style (minimum 4-inch overlap) and seal all laps and end dams with compatible sealant per manufacturer instructions."
  ],
  "rationale": "Unlapped/unsealed flashing violates prescriptive code requirements for weather-resistive barriers and will allow moisture to bypass the drainage plane, leading to premature deterioration of backup materials and potential structural damage.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402/602-16 Section 6.2.2.5.2 (Flashing)",
    "BIA Tech Note 7 (Water Penetration Resistance – Flashing)"
  ]
}
